channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 40, including several kids
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 40 people on Thursday across central and southern Gaza, including an attack on a sprawling tent camp that Israel has repeatedly bombed despite designating it a humanitarian safe zone.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: An aspiring nurse, a football star, a single mother and a father of 2 killed in New Orleans attack
item.description: An 18-year-old woman dreaming of becoming a nurse, a single mother, a father of two and a former Princeton football star were killed when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street, packed with holiday revelers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Governor seeks resignation of Western New Mexico University board after spending revelations
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — New Mexico Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham has asked for the immediate resignation of regents at Western New Mexico State University after revelations of wasteful spending and failures in financial oversight, according to documents released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about the New Year's Day attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden gets the priciest gift from a foreign leader in 2023
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamo
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, KRON4

item.title: Duchess Meghan Netflix series set for January premiere
item.description: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, has a Netflix series set for a mid-January premiere, the streaming service said in a post on the social media platform X on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans terror suspect's brother says attack is sign of 'radicalization': report
item.description: The New Orleans terror suspect's brother says the Bourbon Street attack "does not represent Islam," but instead, "this is more some type of radicalization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US unemployment claims fall to 211,000, the lowest level since March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The number of Americans applying for unemployment checks dropped last week to the lowest level since March, suggesting that suggest most U.S. workers continue to enjoy unusually high job security.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: NYPD presence increased at Trump Tower, Times Square after attacks in New Orleans, Las Vegas
item.description: “While there are no immediate threats to our city at this time, out of an abundance of caution, we have heightened security and have increased NYPD presence at relevant locations"
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Man in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to the head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, KTLA 5

item.title: Duchess Meghan returns to Instagram
item.description: (The Hill) -- Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, announced her return to Instagram on New Year’s Day with a celebratory post on the beach. The former actress Meghan Markle jogged across the sand in an all-white outfit with her hair flowing in the wind before writing 2025 in the sand.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump blames 'open borders' for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump blamed the situation at the U.S.-southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Joe Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: 10 people are wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ten people were wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub while they were waiting to get into a private event, police said. About 15 people were standing outside Amazura nightclub at 11:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYPD says 'not a terrorist attack' after 10 shot outside NYC event space on New Year's Day
item.description: At least 10 people were injured by gunfire late Wednesday at the Amazura nightclub in Jamaica, Queens, New York City police confirmed to Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about victims of New Orleans terrorist attack
item.description: Family and friends have begun identifying the victims after a terrorist suspect plowed a rented truck into a crowd of jubilant New Year's Day celebrators in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Orleans barriers to prevent vehicle attacks were in the process of being replaced
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Seven years ago, New Orleans officials began installing adjustable barriers at intersections in the famed French Quarter to temporarily prevent vehicles from entering the tourist area where the narrow streets are typically teeming with pedestrians every night.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JEFF MARTIN and KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — At least 10 people, including two children, were killed and four others were seriously wounded on Wednesday in a shooting rampage that followed a bar brawl in a western Montenegrin city, officials said. The shooter was on the run.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel in 'intentional' act
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Competency exam set in stabbing of company president
item.description: A man accused of stabbing his boss two weeks ago at a Fruitport Township business had his criminal case put on hold pending a competency exam.
item.dc:creator: John Hogan, NewsNation

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in 20 states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: The FBI said it believes it's the largest amount of homemade explosives it has encountered in its history
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured after Tesla Cybertruck explosion outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas: Police
item.description: Police in Las Vegas responded to Trump Tower on Wednesday morning for reports of an explosion near the front valet entrance to the hotel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Movies in 2024: Lessons from a turbulent year at the box office
item.description: Movie ticket sales took a bit of a hit in 2024. The annual domestic box office is expected to end up at around $8.75 billion, down more than 3% from 2023, according to estimates from Comscore.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans terror attack in photos
item.description: A driver wrought carnage on New Orleans' famed French Quarter early on New Year's Day, killing at least 10 people as he steered around a police blockade and rammed a pickup truck into a crowd before being shot to death by police, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel in 'intentional' act
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things that will shape US politics in 2025
item.description: The fallout of last month’s high-stakes election and recent battles within Congress are offering early clues of what will set the tone for politics in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's is opening a dozen new locations in 2025: Here's where
item.description: The company’s website currently lists 12 planned locations “opening soon” across seven states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, ABC 4

item.title: AP WAS THERE: Carter announces Middle East peace agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — ___ EDITORS: As part of its coverage of the death of former President Jimmy Carter, The Associated Press is looking back at major events in his life. Carter's success at Camp David negotiating a Mideast Peace agreement between Egypt and Israel was one of his crowning achievements.
item.dc:creator: BARRY SCHWEID, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southerners are cleaning up from tornadoes that swept the region
item.description: NEW CANEY, Texas (AP) — Cleanup was underway Monday after a strong storm system spawned hail, rain, high winds and tornadoes across the southern U.S. over the weekend, killing at least four people.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and LEKAN OYEKANMI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, ABC 4
